caption:
🧠 Five brain chips helped a paralyzed man move and feel again.

Keith Thomas was left paralyzed from the chest down after breaking his neck. For years, he couldn’t use his hands. Now, thanks to five tiny brain implants, he’s picking up objects again.

Two of the implants send signals from his brain directly to his arms and hands, bypassing the damaged part of his spinal cord. The other three restore his sense of touch by transmitting signals from sensors on his skin back to his brain.

But here’s the part that stunned researchers: Even after the implants were removed, some of the movement and sensation remained.

Scientists believe the treatment may have encouraged the brain and nervous system to build new pathways, suggesting these implants could eventually do more than temporarily restore function, they might actually help the body relearn how to move.
